req.title: Submit timesheet
req.statement: An employee shall submit a weekly timesheet where each day has hours between 0 and 24 and total week hours are between 0 and 80.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines two independent validation axes: per-day hours [0,24] and weekly total [0,80]. I apply EP to confirm a valid mid-range submission succeeds; BVA to probe the exact boundary values (0, 24, 80) that separate valid from invalid; NEG to confirm per-day hours >24 return 422; DT to enumerate the combined condition table (invalid day AND valid total, valid day AND invalid total); and STC to verify the state transition draft→submitted on a happy-path flow. Each test uses a shared `auth_session` fixture that returns an authenticated requests.Session, and a `reset_timesheet` fixture that deletes any existing draft before the test runs, ensuring idempotency.

tests.name: test_submit_valid_timesheet_positive
preconditions: No existing submitted timesheet for the current week
All daily hours are within valid range (0-24)
Weekly total is within valid range (0-80)
actions: POST /timesheets/submit with 5 workdays each having 8 hours (total=40)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app"
ENDPOINT = "/timesheets/submit"


@pytest.fixture
def auth_session():
    return <fixture_auth_session>


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def reset_timesheet(auth_session):
    <helper_fn_delete_current_week_timesheet>(auth_session)
    yield


def test_submit_valid_timesheet_positive(auth_session):
    payload = {
        "week": <helper_fn_current_iso_week>(),
        "days": [
            {"day": "MON", "hours": 8},
            {"day": "TUE", "hours": 8},
            {"day": "WED", "hours": 8},
            {"day": "THU", "hours": 8},
            {"day": "FRI", "hours": 8},
        ],
    }
    resp = auth_session.post(f"{BASE_URL}{ENDPOINT}", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "submitted"
    assert body["total_hours"] == 40

tests.name: test_submit_per_day_hours_exceeds_24_returns_422
preconditions: No existing submitted timesheet for the current week
actions: POST /timesheets/submit with one day having 25 hours (invalid)
expected: Response body contains validation error referencing per-day hours
trace: R-WEB-067: error pathway 422 when per-day hours outside 0-24

tests.name: test_submit_boundary_max_valid_hours_per_day_and_week
preconditions: No existing submitted timesheet for the current week
actions: POST /timesheets/submit with MON=24, TUE=24, WED=24, THU=8, FRI=0 (total=80, all days at or within boundary)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app"
ENDPOINT = "/timesheets/submit"


@pytest.fixture
def auth_session():
    return <fixture_auth_session>


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def reset_timesheet(auth_session):
    <helper_fn_delete_current_week_timesheet>(auth_session)
    yield


def test_submit_boundary_max_valid_hours_per_day_and_week(auth_session):
    payload = {
        "week": <helper_fn_current_iso_week>(),
        "days": [
            {"day": "MON", "hours": 24},
            {"day": "TUE", "hours": 24},
            {"day": "WED", "hours": 24},
            {"day": "THU", "hours": 8},
            {"day": "FRI", "hours": 0},
        ],
    }
    resp = auth_session.post(f"{BASE_URL}{ENDPOINT}", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "submitted"
    assert body["total_hours"] == 80

tests.name: test_submit_weekly_total_exceeds_80_returns_422
preconditions: No existing submitted timesheet for the current week
Each individual day has hours <= 24 (per-day rule satisfied)
Weekly total exceeds 80 (weekly total rule violated)
actions: POST /timesheets/submit with 5 days x 17 hours each = 85 hours total, each day within 0-24
expected: Response body contains validation error referencing weekly total
trace: R-WEB-067: error pathway 422 when weekly total outside 0-80
R-WEB-067: DT condition — valid per-day, invalid weekly total

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_state_transitions_draft_to_submitted
preconditions: A draft timesheet exists for the current week with valid hours
actions: Create a draft timesheet via POST /timesheets/draft
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app"
DRAFT_ENDPOINT = "/timesheets/draft"
SUBMIT_ENDPOINT = "/timesheets/submit"
CURRENT_ENDPOINT = "/timesheets/current"


@pytest.fixture
def auth_session():
    return <fixture_auth_session>


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def reset_timesheet(auth_session):
    <helper_fn_delete_current_week_timesheet>(auth_session)
    yield


def test_submit_timesheet_state_transitions_draft_to_submitted(auth_session):
    week = <helper_fn_current_iso_week>()
    days = [
        {"day": "MON", "hours": 8},
        {"day": "TUE", "hours": 8},
        {"day": "WED", "hours": 8},
        {"day": "THU", "hours": 8},
        {"day": "FRI", "hours": 8},
    ]
    draft_resp = auth_session.post(f"{BASE_URL}{DRAFT_ENDPOINT}", json={"week": week, "days": days})
    assert draft_resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    assert draft_resp.json()["state"] == "draft"
    submit_resp = auth_session.post(f"{BASE_URL}{SUBMIT_ENDPOINT}", json={"week": week})
    assert submit_resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    get_resp = auth_session.get(f"{BASE_URL}{CURRENT_ENDPOINT}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["state"] == "submitted"

tests.name: test_submit_boundary_zero_hours_all_days
preconditions: No existing submitted timesheet for the current week
actions: POST /timesheets/submit with all 5 days having 0 hours (lower boundary, total=0)

            {"day": "FRI", "hours": 0},
        ],

    resp = auth_session.post(f"{BASE_URL}{ENDPOINT}", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "submitted"
    assert body["total_hours"] == 0
